headings.description: Premium, confident, and editorial. DO NOT use generic sans-serif fonts for headings.

body.description: Clean, highly legible geometric sans for perfect contrast with the serif headlines.

primary.description: Deep navy blue. Used for prominent backgrounds, major headings, and the final CTA band.

accent.description: Gold. Used strictly for CTAs, important highlights, and the 'Most Popular' badge.

textures.paper: Use a subtle CSS noise or the provided white paper texture image at 3-5% opacity over off-white backgrounds to kill digital flatness.

sections.nav: Sticky top nav (logo left, links center, CTA right). Solid white or glassmorphism background to ensure scroll text does not bleed through.
sections.hero: Two-column asymmetrical or centered with constrained max-width (max-w-3xl). Generous white space. Hero title in Playfair Display.
sections.product_ladder: 4-column grid on desktop (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4). The 3rd card must be scaled up slightly (scale-105 on desktop) with a Gold border and a distinct 'Most Popular' badge.
sections.features: 3-column CSS Grid. Use lucide-react icons in a Gold/Navy circular wrapper. Left-align text for readability.
sections.testimonials: Masonry or 3-column grid. Clean white cards on an off-white background.
sections.faq: Max-width constrained (max-w-3xl) accordion centered on page. Use lucide-react plus/minus icons.
sections.final_cta: Full-width deep navy background. Gold text. Centered text alignment, high contrast.

motion.hover_states: Buttons translate-y-[-2px] with increased shadow. Cards translate-y-[-4px]. Use transition-all duration-300 ease-out.
motion.entrances: Fade up (translate-y-8, opacity-0 to opacity-100) on scroll using framer-motion. Stagger children for grid items.

hero_background_abstract.description: Dark blue abstract gradient background. Use with low opacity (10-20%) blending into the navy background for the Final CTA band.

gold_texture.description: Abstract golden rectangles. Can be used as a mask or very subtle background detail in the 'Most Popular' card.

paper_texture.description: Minimalist white paper texture. Overlay on the entire site background with mix-blend-multiply and 2% opacity to add editorial print feel.

components.buttons: Pill-shaped (rounded-full). Primary uses Gold (#B8860B) background with White text. Hover state slightly darker gold (#986F09). Secondary is transparent with Navy border.
components.cards: Flat, pure white (#FFFFFF), 1px border (#E2E8F0), rounded-2xl. Padding inside should be generous (p-8).
components.badge: For 'Most Popular': position absolute, top-0, translate-y-[-50%]. Background Gold (#B8860B), text White, bold, uppercase, tracking-widest, text-xs, rounded-full, px-4 py-1.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure Gold (#B8860B) text on White passes APCA. If not, darken Gold slightly for text, but keep brand Gold for large buttons.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='hero-cta-button').

instructions_to_main_agent: Use Playfair Display for all h1, h2, h3 tags and Plus Jakarta Sans for body text.
Avoid AI slop: NO purple gradients, NO 'Inter' font, NO centered cards with drop shadows everywhere.
Implement the 'Most Popular' pricing card by scaling it to 1.05 on md+ screens, giving it a border of #B8860B, and adding the Gold badge on top.
For the Localisation Feature Grid (Why this is different), left-align the content. Use Lucide icons (size 24 or 32, strokeWidth 1.5) with a navy or gold background circle.
In the Social Proof section, do not use photos of people. Use the user's initials (e.g., 'OA') in an elegant circular avatar with a Navy background and Gold text.
Ensure the Navigation bar is sticky and uses a solid white or heavily blurred glass background so text does not clash with scrolling content.
Do not use generic robot or human photos. The design should rely heavily on its typography, grid, spacing, and brand colors to feel expensive.
Build standard React (.js) components. Do not use Typescript (.tsx).
All components should be customized Shadcn elements or pure Tailwind HTML if Shadcn doesn't fit the editorial layout.
Use Naira currency (₦) for all pricing points.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed.
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones.
Use sonner for toasts, located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.js.
